steps.title: Translate the phrase into an expression
steps.content: "15 less than 8 times $n$" means $8n - 15$. This is the minimum value of $x$.

steps.title: Write the inequality
steps.content: If $8n - 15$ is the minimum value of $x$, then $x$ must be greater than or equal to it: $x \ge 8n - 15$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• A. $x \le 8n - 15$ — this would make $8n - 15$ the maximum, not the minimum; incorrect.
  • B. $x \ge 8n - 15$ — matches our derivation.
  • C. $x \le 15 - 8n$ — wrong expression and wrong direction; incorrect.
  • D. $x \ge 15 - 8n$ — the expression $15 - 8n$ reverses the phrase; incorrect.
That confirms B is correct.
